## Delft Circuits Appoints Karen Keenan as COO to Scale Quantum I/O Hardware Manufacturing for Global Customers
Maarten Hendriks to Lead Quantum Fab and Infrastructure Expansion 
  * 07/04/2026


## // NEWS ARTICLE
## Delft Circuits Appoints Karen Keenan as COO to Scale Quantum I/O Hardware Manufacturing for Global Customers
Maarten Hendriks to Lead Quantum Fab and Infrastructure Expansion 
**DELFT, Netherlands (April 7, 2026)** – Delft Circuits today announces the appointment of  
Karen Keenan as Chief Operating Officer (COO), marking a key step in scaling the company  
into a global quantum I/O hardware manufacturing platform. Karen succeeds Maarten  
Hendriks, who will transition into a new role focused on leading the expansion of the  
company’s Quantum Fab infrastructure in the Netherlands as the company accelerates  
production capabilities to meet increasing demand.  
This leadership transition comes at a pivotal moment for Delft Circuits. The company is  
advancing the development of its new Quantum Fab in Delft, a dedicated facility for  
manufacturing quantum I/O hardware, while also planning further expansion of its global  
commercial and field operations to support customers worldwide. Together, these efforts  
position Delft Circuits well to support the scaling of the next generation of quantum  
systems.  
“We are entering a new phase of our growth, where scaling operations becomes just as  
important as advancing the technology itself. I am excited to join at this key moment and to  
work with the team building the operational backbone required for our global growth, while  
preserving the quality and innovation that define the company,” said Karen Keenan, COO at  
Delft Circuits.  
As the quantum industry continues to scale, Delft Circuits is rapidly expanding its production  
capabilities, operational bandwidth and global footprint to meet increasing customer  
demand.  
“We have built a strong operational foundation over the past years, and the next step will  
help us scale it even further. Karen brings exactly the right leadership and experience  
needed for this phase. I am excited to now focus fully on expanding our Quantum Fab and  
infrastructure in the Netherlands, ensuring we are ready to support the growing demand for  
quantum I/O hardware on a global level,” added Maarten Hendriks.  
This transition further strengthens Delft Circuits’ executive team, following the recent  
appointment of Martin Danoesastro as CEO and the earlier addition of CCO Robby  
Ferdinandus. Together, these changes reflect the company’s continued evolution toward  
industrial-scale operations and its ambition to play a central role serving customers in the  
global quantum ecosystem.  
CEO Martin Danoesastro added “Our customers are rapidly scaling their quantum systems,  
driving a new level of demand for reliable, industrial-grade I/O hardware. The next step is to  
industrialize that scale globally. Karen brings the experience required to scale our operations  
to meet increasing global demand and drive execution at speed, while Maarten focuses on  
expanding our Quantum Fab and infrastructure. In parallel, we are strengthening our global  
customer-facing capabilities to serve them at scale. Together, this leadership team positions  
Delft Circuits to accelerate growth, attract top talent, and partner with the leading players  
shaping the future of quantum systems.”  
Karen Keenan brings a strong combination of operational excellence, strategic insight, and  
people-focused leadership. She holds both a bachelor’s and a master’s degree in aerospace  
engineering from TU Delft and began her career at Boston Consulting Group (BCG), where  
she developed a strong foundation in business strategy. She later joined Luuna (now  
Zebrands) in Mexico as Head of Product at an early stage. As COO, she played a key role in  
scaling the organization from 35 to over 800 employees, growing revenues from $5 million  
to over $100 million, significantly improving customer satisfaction, and achieving positive  
EBITDA.  
With Karen Keenan as COO and Maarten Hendriks leading infrastructure expansion, Delft  
Circuits is entering its next phase focused on scaling production, advancing its Quantum Fab  
infrastructure, and supporting the rapid growth of the quantum industry worldwide.
**About Delft Circuits**  
Delft Circuits is a leading provider of quantum hardware, dedicated to supplying the best  
hardware for the quantum industry. With a focus on designing and developing I/O cabling  
solutions, the company is a trusted partner for blue-chip corporations, leading national  
laboratories, and academics. With a beachhead market in the quantum industry, Delft  
Circuits has already delivered hundreds of I/O modules for a wide range of customers. As an  
ambitious, independent quantum infrastructure supplier, the company is committed to  
pioneering I/O for advanced technologies. Investors in Delft Circuits include Deeptech XL,  
QuVest Capital, Scholt Group, High Tech Gründerfonds (HTGF), Waves Capital Partners and  
Paeonia Group. For more information visit: www.delft-circuits.com

					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Delft Circuits Appoints Karen Keenan as COO to Scale Quantum I/O Hardware Manufacturing for Global Customers</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-aad42e3 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="aad42e3" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">

		<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-3f4fcee e-con-full e-flex e-con e-child" data-id="3f4fcee" data-element_type="container" data-e-type="container">
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-ce41a8d elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="ce41a8d" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">

				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-800e177 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="800e177" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Delft Circuits Appoints Karen Keenan as COO to Scale Quantum I/O Hardware Manufacturing for Global Customers</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-4f9f572 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="4f9f572" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Maarten Hendriks to Lead Quantum Fab and Infrastructure Expansion 								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-b2f6aec elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="b2f6aec" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									<p><strong>DELFT, Netherlands (April 7, 2026)</strong> – Delft Circuits today announces the appointment of<br>
Karen Keenan as Chief Operating Officer (COO), marking a key step in scaling the company<br>
into a global quantum I/O hardware manufacturing platform. Karen succeeds Maarten<br>
Hendriks, who will transition into a new role focused on leading the expansion of the<br>
company’s Quantum Fab infrastructure in the Netherlands as the company accelerates<br>
production capabilities to meet increasing demand.<br>
This leadership transition comes at a pivotal moment for Delft Circuits. The company is<br>
advancing the development of its new Quantum Fab in Delft, a dedicated facility for<br>
manufacturing quantum I/O hardware, while also planning further expansion of its global<br>
commercial and field operations to support customers worldwide. Together, these efforts<br>
position Delft Circuits well to support the scaling of the next generation of quantum<br>
systems.<br>
“We are entering a new phase of our growth, where scaling operations becomes just as<br>
important as advancing the technology itself. I am excited to join at this key moment and to<br>
work with the team building the operational backbone required for our global growth, while<br>
preserving the quality and innovation that define the company,” said Karen Keenan, COO at<br>
Delft Circuits.<br>
As the quantum industry continues to scale, Delft Circuits is rapidly expanding its production<br>
capabilities, operational bandwidth and global footprint to meet increasing customer<br>
demand.<br>
“We have built a strong operational foundation over the past years, and the next step will<br>
help us scale it even further. Karen brings exactly the right leadership and experience<br>
needed for this phase. I am excited to now focus fully on expanding our Quantum Fab and<br>
infrastructure in the Netherlands, ensuring we are ready to support the growing demand for<br>
quantum I/O hardware on a global level,” added Maarten Hendriks.<br>
This transition further strengthens Delft Circuits’ executive team, following the recent<br>
appointment of Martin Danoesastro as CEO and the earlier addition of CCO Robby<br>
Ferdinandus. Together, these changes reflect the company’s continued evolution toward<br>
industrial-scale operations and its ambition to play a central role serving customers in the<br>
global quantum ecosystem.<br>
CEO Martin Danoesastro added “Our customers are rapidly scaling their quantum systems,<br>
driving a new level of demand for reliable, industrial-grade I/O hardware. The next step is to<br>
industrialize that scale globally. Karen brings the experience required to scale our operations<br>
to meet increasing global demand and drive execution at speed, while Maarten focuses on<br>
expanding our Quantum Fab and infrastructure. In parallel, we are strengthening our global<br>
customer-facing capabilities to serve them at scale. Together, this leadership team positions<br>
Delft Circuits to accelerate growth, attract top talent, and partner with the leading players<br>
shaping the future of quantum systems.”<br>
Karen Keenan brings a strong combination of operational excellence, strategic insight, and<br>
people-focused leadership. She holds both a bachelor’s and a master’s degree in aerospace<br>
engineering from TU Delft and began her career at Boston Consulting Group (BCG), where<br>
she developed a strong foundation in business strategy. She later joined Luuna (now<br>
Zebrands) in Mexico as Head of Product at an early stage. As COO, she played a key role in<br>
scaling the organization from 35 to over 800 employees, growing revenues from $5 million<br>
to over $100 million, significantly improving customer satisfaction, and achieving positive<br>
EBITDA.<br>
With Karen Keenan as COO and Maarten Hendriks leading infrastructure expansion, Delft<br>
Circuits is entering its next phase focused on scaling production, advancing its Quantum Fab<br>
infrastructure, and supporting the rapid growth of the quantum industry worldwide.</p>
<p><strong>About Delft Circuits</strong><br>
Delft Circuits is a leading provider of quantum hardware, dedicated to supplying the best<br>
hardware for the quantum industry. With a focus on designing and developing I/O cabling<br>
solutions, the company is a trusted partner for blue-chip corporations, leading national<br>
laboratories, and academics. With a beachhead market in the quantum industry, Delft<br>
Circuits has already delivered hundreds of I/O modules for a wide range of customers. As an<br>
ambitious, independent quantum infrastructure supplier, the company is committed to<br>
pioneering I/O for advanced technologies. Investors in Delft Circuits include Deeptech XL,<br>
QuVest Capital, Scholt Group, High Tech Gründerfonds (HTGF), Waves Capital Partners and<br>
Paeonia Group. For more information visit: www.delft-circuits.com</p>
